commit.message:
Fix [AUDIT-004] store_quorum_set now unblocks fetching envelopes

store_quorum_set only updated ScpDriver and the quorum tracker, leaving
FetchingEnvelopes unaware of newly stored quorum sets. Envelopes blocked
waiting for a quorum set would never get unblocked through the app
consensus handler path (which calls store_quorum_set + clear_quorum_set_request
but not recv_quorum_set).

Mirror the quorum set receipt into FetchingEnvelopes at the end of
store_quorum_set so blocked envelopes get unblocked.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
Fix double-apply of fee/seq/signer on cluster op failure in pre-parallel path

When pre_parallel_apply commits fee/seq/signer mutations to the main delta,
and a cluster's apply_body later fails, rollback_failed_tx was calling
restore_delta_entries which re-recorded those same mutations on the cluster
delta. Merging the cluster delta back into the main delta would then
double-apply them.

Fix: clear the rollback DeltaEntries in execute_with_pre_apply_result before
calling apply_body. On operation failure, restore_delta_entries sees empty
lists and becomes a no-op — matching stellar-core's parallelApply which
simply sets txFAILED without touching fee/seq/signer state (already
committed by preParallelApply on the shared ledger txn).

Meta generation (tx_changes_before, fee_changes) is unaffected — those are
separate fields in PreApplyResult.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
Fix IndexError and KeyError in test_buffer_pg for ports without 3-4 PG (#23312)

### Description of PR

Fix IndexError and KeyError in test_buffer_pg when ports don't have
BUFFER_PG|{port}|3-4 configured.

### Type of change

- [x] Bug fix

### Back port request
- [ ] 202205
- [ ] 202305
- [ ] 202311
- [ ] 202405
- [ ] 202411
- [ ] 202505
- [ ] 202511

### Approach
#### What is the motivation for this PR?
On some HW SKUs (e.g. Arista-7260CX3-C64), certain ports do not have
`BUFFER_PG|{port}|3-4` configured in CONFIG_DB, and their PG entries may
be missing from `COUNTERS_PG_NAME_MAP`. This causes:
1. `IndexError: list index out of range` when accessing
`profile_in_pg[0]` on an empty result (on internal branch with
`dut_asic.run_redis_cmd()`)
2. `KeyError` when accessing `pg_name_map[port:pg]` for missing PG
entries

Kusto analysis shows this consistently fails on Arista-7260CX3-C64
(bjw2-can-7260-12, bjw-can-7260-8) and occasionally on NH-5010-F-O64.

#### How did you do it?
- Added empty check for `profile_in_pg` before accessing its elements
- Added key existence check for `pg_name_map` before dictionary access
(both admin-up and admin-down paths)
- Logs informational message when a PG is not found in
COUNTERS_PG_NAME_MAP and skips ASIC_DB check for that PG

#### How did you verify/test it?
Code review and Kusto analysis of failure patterns.

#### Any platform specific information?
Affects Arista-7260CX3-C64 and NH-5010-F-O64.

ADO PBI: #37230137

Fix [AUDIT-095] pre-parallel apply state isolation for Soroban clusters

Implement pre_parallel_apply to globalize classic account mutations
(sequence bumps, signer removals) before parallel cluster execution.
This matches stellar-core's preParallelApplyAndCollectModifiedClassicEntries
which runs preParallelApply for ALL Soroban TXs on shared state before
any cluster starts.

Before: each cluster ran pre_apply_arc locally, so peer clusters saw
stale sequence numbers and signers for accounts modified by other clusters.

After: pre_parallel_apply runs all pre-apply mutations on a shared executor,
transfers them to the main delta, then clusters only run apply_body.

Changes:
- Add pre_parallel_apply() in tx_set.rs: shared executor runs pre_apply_arc
  for all Soroban TXs, stores PreParallelResult per TX, applies to delta
- Add PreParallelResult enum: Success(PreApplyResult) | Failed(result)
- Add execute_with_pre_apply_result() on TransactionExecutor
- Modify execute_single_cluster to accept pre-computed results
- Make pre_apply_arc and build_skipped_result pub(super) for tx_set access
- Add test: test_audit_095_pre_parallel_apply_globalizes_seq_bumps

fix: correct digest canonical serialization in load_share_input (v0.4.59)

The spec_digest was being computed from pretty-printed raw JSON bytes in
load_share_input(), but build_share_lock() uses serde_json::to_vec() which
produces compact, deterministic bytes. This caused false-positive digest
mismatch warnings on valid local share.spec.json / share.lock.json files.

Fix: use serde_json::to_vec(&spec) for digest computation in both the
spec-path and lock-path branches of load_share_input(), matching
build_share_lock() exactly.

Also fixes the T9 test fixture to use canonical digest computation, and
adds T8b/T8c tests confirming valid digest passes for both load paths.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>
